uttaksplan.periodeListeContent.familiehe: If {erFarEllerMedmor, select, true {{navnMor}} other {you}} give birth before term, {erFarEllerMedmor, select, true {{navnMor}} other {you}} start using the quota {erFarEllerMedmor, select, true {her} other {your}} from birth. The rest of the three weeks will be deleted. If {erFarEllerMedmor, select, true {{navnMor}} other {you}} give birth after term, {erFarEllerMedmor, select, true {{navnMor}} other {you}} will use the shared period until the birth.

Mother gets 3 weeks of parental benefits set aside to be used before term. If the baby is born before term, the days from this period will be lost, and the mother will start using the maternity quota from the day the baby is born.
If the baby is born after term, the mother will use some days from the shared period before the birth, but will not start using the maternity quota until after the baby is born.

However, in some cases, you can apply to take over the {erMedmor, select, true {co-mother's} other {father's}} quota.
The mother's quota is ordinarily reserved for the mother.

LeggTilPauseForm.Heading: Activity requirements apply in this period
LeggTilPauseForm.Aktivitetskrav: If you later want to take parental benefits with activity requirements, we need to know what the mother is doing during this period.
UforutsetteEndringer.UforutsetteEndringe: If you become sick during your period with parental benefit
The other parent can take over the period with parental benefit when one is sick. The parent who is sick usually receives sickness benefit instead of parental benefit.
If you are too sick to take care of the child, you can usually receive sickness benefit instead of parental benefit during the period of illness.
If you have a new child before three years have passed

You must withdraw the parental benefit before the child turns 3 years old, or before a new parental benefit period starts for a new child. The remaining days of parental benefit for the first child will normally lapse. There are exceptions if there are 48 weeks or less between births or adoptions.
If {erAlene, select, true {you} other {the mother}} becomes sick in the first six weeks of parental benefit

The first six weeks after birth are reserved for the {erMedmor, select, true {mother giving birth} other {mother}}, and it is generally not possible to move them to a later date. The exception is if the {erMedmor, select, true {mother giving birth} other {mother}} giving birth becomes sick and cannot care for the child. Then the {hvem} can take over the period or the {erMedmor, select, true {mother who gives birth} other {mother}} can apply to defer parental benefits.

The first six weeks after birth are reserved for the mother, and it is generally not possible to move them to a later date. The exception is if the mother becomes sick and cannot care for the child. You can then apply to defer your parental benefits and receive sick pay instead of parental benefits.

During the first six weeks after birth, if the mother becomes sick or is hospitalized and cannot care for the child, the father or co-mother can apply for parental benefits with activity requirements.
If the child is sick after the first six weeks
If a child is ill or hospitalized, you may be entitled to care allowance instead of parental allowance.
If the child is sick or admitted to hospital
If your child is ill or hospitalized, you may be entitled to care allowance for caring for a sick child.
If the child is admitted to hospital during the first six weeks

If your child is ill or hospitalized during the first six weeks after birth, you may be entitled to care allowance for caring for a sick child instead of parental allowance. In order to defer parental allowance until later, the child must be hospitalized.
The baby is born before 33 weeks of pregnancy

The period of parental benefit is extended from the date of birth until the day before the due date. With this extension, you will receive parental benefit for the same length of time as if the child had been born on the due date. Care allowance before the due date will be deducted from the days of extended parental benefit entitlement and cannot be taken later.

Parental benefits with activity requirements are extended from week 7 after birth until the day before the due date. If you receive care allowance during the extended period, the days of parental allowance will be deducted and cannot be taken later. The {erMedmor, select, true {mother who gives birth} other {mother}} must meet the activity requirement when you want to take or postpone the days until later.
HvaErMulig.Tittel: What is possible when you customize?
HvaErMulig.MyeDuKanEndre: There is a lot that can be changed in the plan.
HvaErMulig.FarFellesperiode: When the {hvem} takes the joint period
HvaErMulig.FarFellesperiode.Adopsjon: When {erFedre, select, true {father 2} other {{erMedmor, select, true {mother 2} other {the father}}}} takes common period
HvaErMulig.FarFellesperiode.Tekst: The {erMedmor, select, true {mother who gives birth} other {mother}} must be actively employed, which means that {erMorHovedsøker, select, true {she} other {he}} must, for example, be working or studying. This must be documented by the {erMedmor, select, true {co-mother} other {father}} in their application.
HvaErMulig.FarFellesperiode.Tekst.Adopsj: {erFedre, select, true {Father 1} other {{erMedmor, select, true {Mother 1} other {The mother}}}} must be employed, which means that {erMorHovedsøker, select, true {she} other {he}} must, for example, be working or studying. This must be documented in the application by {erFedre, select, true {father 2} other {{erMedmor, select, true {mother 2} other {the father}}}}.
HvaErMulig.LeggeTilFerie.Tekst: If {erAlenesøker, select, true {you're not} other {neither parent is}} going to receive parental benefits, {erAlenesøker, select, true {you} other {one}} can add periods without parental benefits, where, for example, {erAlenesøker, select, true {you} other {one}} receives holiday pay from the employer.
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerUtenAktivitetsk: These are weeks without any requirement that {erMedmor, select, true {mother who gives birth} other {mother}} is working. You can take the weeks off at any time before the child turns three, or before you start a new parental benefit period for a new child. You can start at the earliest two weeks before the due date.
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerMedAktivitetskr: The {erMedmor, select, true {mother who gives birth} other {mother}} must be in approved activity. The period of activity requirements starts when the child is 7 weeks old. Periods when the {erMedmor, select, true {mother who gives birth} other {mother}} is not in approved activity are days you can lose. The {erMedmor, select, true {mother who gives birth} other {mother}} must be in approved activity when you are to receive or postpone parental benefits until later. You do not lose parental benefits with activity requirements during periods when you have parental benefits without activity requirements.
HvaErMulig.ManKanJobbeSamtidig: You can work part-time while receiving parental benefits partially, the period with parental benefits will then last longer.
HvaErMulig.SeksUkerEtterFødsel.DeFørsteU: The first six weeks after birth are reserved for the mother and locked to this time.
HvaErMulig.SeksUkerEtterFødsel.OfteFårFe: Often, {erFar, select, true {fathers} other {mothers}} get leave covered for the first two weeks after birth by their employer. If {hvem} does not receive this, {erFar, select, true {he} other {she}} can use their own quota during these two weeks.
HvaErMulig.TreUkerFørTermin: Three weeks before due date: The mother has three weeks that can only be used before the birth. If the mother gives birth before the due date, she begins to use her quota from the birth. The remaining of the three weeks are lost and cannot be used after the birth. If the mother gives birth after the due date, she uses the shared period until the birth.
HvaErMulig.ToUkerRundtFødsel.Tekst: Often {hvem}s have leave covered for the first two weeks after birth by their employer. If not, the {hvem} can use parental benefit in these two weeks.
HvaErMulig.ToUkerRundtFødsel.FedreTekst: Fathers often receive paid leave from their employer for the first two weeks after the birth. If you do not receive this, you can apply for parental benefits instead.
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig: Parental benefits at the same time
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig.Tekst: You can receive parental benefits at the same time as described here. This is possible from the time when you are both entitled to parental benefits.
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig.Forskj: You can receive parental benefits in different ways at the same time
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig.ToUker: 2 weeks around birth: If the {erFar, select, true {father} other {co-mother}} wishes, {erFar, select, true {he} other {she}} can use their own quota during these two weeks, while the {erFar, select, true {mother} other {mother giving birth}} receives parental benefits. This is possible up to 2 weeks before the due date and 6 weeks after the birth.
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig.Flerba: Multiple child days up to 200%: If you have twins or more children, you can receive parental benefits at the same time. The period during which you can receive parental benefits at the same time is called multiple child days. This is possible at any time during the parental benefit period. You can choose your own quota or a joint period.

Multiple child days up to 200%: You can take the extra days you are entitled to at the same time. This is possible at any time during the parental benefit period. You can choose between your own quota or a joint period.
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig.Opptil: Up to 100%: If you want to receive less than 100% parental benefit in total, you must both work part-time.{erAdopsjon, select, true {} other { This is possible from week 7 after the birth.}}
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig.TilSam: Total 100%: If you have a total of 100%, you can choose whether to work during the period when you are not receiving parental benefits. The exception is if the {erFar, select, true {father} other {co-mother}} is to have a joint period. In that case, the {erFar, select, true {mother} other {mother who gives birth}} must work to the same extent as the {erFar, select, true {father} other {co-mother}} is to have a shared period.{erAdopsjon, select, true {} other { This is possible from week 7 after the birth.}}
HvaErMulig.ForeldrepengerSamtidig.Opptil: Up to 150%: If one parent uses up to 50% of the shared period and works 50%, while the other uses their own quota up to 100%. If the parent who takes their own quota is to have less than 100%, that parent must work part-time.{erAdopsjon, select, true {} other { This is possible from week 7 after the birth.}}
HvaErMulig.Omsorgspermisjon.Tekst: When you adopt, you may be entitled to two weeks of paid family care leave from your employer. If you do not receive this, you can apply for parental benefits instead.
HvaErMulig.AktivitetskravTilFar: Activity requirements for the biological fathers
HvaErMulig.AktivitetskravTilFar.Tekst: The biological father must meet the activity requirement if the father who adopts a stepchild takes more than 15 or 19 weeks of parental leave. There is no activity requirement for fathers who adopt stepchildren.
